title: Unable to use Avahi in Ubuntu Xenial running in podman
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running Ubuntu Xenial in podman, announcing a service with Avahi appears to do nothing although a success message appears. The service cannot be found - inside the same container - with avahi-browse.

Note that I am absolutely uncertain whether this is a bug in podman or whether it's simply related to the sheer age of Ubuntu Xenial.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

    $ podman run -ti ubuntu:xenial

then in the running container:

    root@94d60fa09c1c:/# apt update && apt install avahi-utils -y
    [...]
    root@94d60fa09c1c:/# service dbus start
    * Starting system message bus dbus                         [OK]
    root@94d60fa09c1c:/# service avahi-daemon start
    * Starting Avahi mDNS/DNS-SD Daemon avahi-daemon           [OK]
    root@94d60fa09c1c:/# avahi-publish-service somename _foo._tcp 54321
    Established under name 'somename'

then back in the host system, in a separate terminal:

    podman exec -ti 94d60fa09c1c /bin/bash
    root@94d60fa09c1c:/# avahi-browse -a

There is no output.

**Describe the results you received:**

avahi-browse does not discover the announced service.

**Describe the results you expected:**

avahi-browse should discover the announced service.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

It works perfectly with Ubuntu Bionic or later.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 100:3.4.2-1 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 100:3.4.2-1 arm64
podman/unknown 100:3.4.2-1 armhf

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Tested with the latest version of Podman: No
Checked the troubleshooting guide: Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Podman runs inside a VirtualBox VM on Linux Mint 20.3 Una, which is based on Ubuntu Focal.

comments.body: Avahi is multicast, if I'm correct? I don't believe slirp4netns handles multicast, so you'll probably need to use `--net=host`.
